title: poa nemoralis var. interior
intro: Poa nemoralis var. interior, also known as mountain bluegrass, is a type of grass native to North America, characterized by its bright green foliage and ability to thrive in high-altitude regions. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. The medicinal properties of Poa nemoralis var. interior are said to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting relaxation and sleep. Herbal preparations of Poa nemoralis var. interior include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
